LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO JULY-30-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS AS TO CLAIMS AGAINST IT IS GRANTED (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Lyft, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings as to claims against it is granted (see word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Continued To Aug-13-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
